2	name: cbmc-editormovecursor
3	description: editorMoveCursor verifies 34/61; depth-200 global-init ceiling caps deeper-path kills; exact cx/coloff ensures backfire
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 843b5dd0-ef59-4c3e-b762-19f18ce4be53
8	---
9	
10	editorMoveCursor (kilo.c, no callees) verifies and scores **34/61** at canonical
11	`--partial-loops --unwind 5` + `--depth 200`. Up from 0 (no contract existed).
12	
13	Model: `E.numrows==2`, `is_fresh(E.row, sizeof(erow)*2)`, all of
14	cx/coloff/screencols/screenrows/row[i].size bounded to [0,1000] (or [1,1000]),
15	and rowoff,cy each in [0,numrows] with `rowoff+cy<=numrows` (memory safety for
16	`E.row[filerow]` and `E.row[filerow-1]`). `assigns(E.cx,E.cy,E.rowoff,E.coloff)`.
17	**Overflow pitfall:** must bound rowoff AND cy individually *before* the
18	`rowoff+cy<=numrows` requires — CBMC checks overflow on the `rowoff+cy`
19	expression inside the requires itself.
20	
21	Winning ensures (cheap + strong): `cx>=0`; `coloff+cx <= rowlen_final`; **exact
22	final `rowoff+cy` (filerow)** and **exact final `rowoff`** via `__CPROVER_old`
23	history macros keyed on `key` (clamp never touches rowoff/cy, so the switch
24	fully determines them). These 4 give 34.
25	
26	**Key lesson — expensive ensures backfire:** adding the *full* exact `E.cx` /
27	`E.coloff` functional postcondition (folding in the end-of-line clamp) DROPPED
28	the score 34→23. The giant nested-ternary ensures evaluation consumes the shared
29	`--depth 200` budget, truncating body paths *before* they reach the assertions,
30	so fewer mutants are observed. Cheaper, well-chosen ensures beat exhaustive ones.
31	
32	**Ceiling is structural, not spec-strength:** ~6-7 survivors are provably
33	equivalent given the model (filerow in [0,2]: `>0`≡`!=0`, `>=numrows`≡`==numrows`,
34	`<numrows`≡`!=numrows`; clamp `filecol>rowlen` vs `>=` and `cx<0` vs `<=0` are
35	no-ops at the boundary). The other ~20 (ARROW_RIGHT/LEFT-inner cx/coloff split,
36	cy==screenrows-1, clamp arithmetic) are **killable only at --depth >=300**
37	(directly confirmed: L2018 ARROW_DOWN cy-mutant SUCCESS@200, FAILED@300). The
38	syntax-DB global init (C_HL_keywords etc.) eats most of the 200-step budget;
39	shrinking to numrows=1 does NOT free enough (still survives @200) and makes more
40	mutants equivalent — not worth it. Same depth-200 cap pattern as siblings
41	[[cbmc-editorreadkey]], [[cbmc-editorinsertchar]], [[cbmc-editorinsertnewline]].
42	
43	Scorer: `/app/score_emc.py` (patches each mutant line onto kilo.c, runs the
44	canonical pipeline, no callee replacement needed). Keep it (don't delete).

---
name: cbmc-editormovecursor
description: editorMoveCursor verifies 34/61; depth-200 global-init ceiling caps deeper-path kills; exact cx/coloff ensures backfire
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 843b5dd0-ef59-4c3e-b762-19f18ce4be53
---

editorMoveCursor (kilo.c, no callees) verifies and scores **34/61** at canonical
`--partial-loops --unwind 5` + `--depth 200`. Up from 0 (no contract existed).

Model: `E.numrows==2`, `is_fresh(E.row, sizeof(erow)*2)`, all of
cx/coloff/screencols/screenrows/row[i].size bounded to [0,1000] (or [1,1000]),
and rowoff,cy each in [0,numrows] with `rowoff+cy<=numrows` (memory safety for
`E.row[filerow]` and `E.row[filerow-1]`). `assigns(E.cx,E.cy,E.rowoff,E.coloff)`.
**Overflow pitfall:** must bound rowoff AND cy individually *before* the
`rowoff+cy<=numrows` requires — CBMC checks overflow on the `rowoff+cy`
expression inside the requires itself.

Winning ensures (cheap + strong): `cx>=0`; `coloff+cx <= rowlen_final`; **exact
final `rowoff+cy` (filerow)** and **exact final `rowoff`** via `__CPROVER_old`
history macros keyed on `key` (clamp never touches rowoff/cy, so the switch
fully determines them). These 4 give 34.

**Key lesson — expensive ensures backfire:** adding the *full* exact `E.cx` /
`E.coloff` functional postcondition (folding in the end-of-line clamp) DROPPED
the score 34→23. The giant nested-ternary ensures evaluation consumes the shared
`--depth 200` budget, truncating body paths *before* they reach the assertions,
so fewer mutants are observed. Cheaper, well-chosen ensures beat exhaustive ones.

**Ceiling is structural, not spec-strength:** ~6-7 survivors are provably
equivalent given the model (filerow in [0,2]: `>0`≡`!=0`, `>=numrows`≡`==numrows`,
`<numrows`≡`!=numrows`; clamp `filecol>rowlen` vs `>=` and `cx<0` vs `<=0` are
no-ops at the boundary). The other ~20 (ARROW_RIGHT/LEFT-inner cx/coloff split,
cy==screenrows-1, clamp arithmetic) are **killable only at --depth >=300**
(directly confirmed: L2018 ARROW_DOWN cy-mutant SUCCESS@200, FAILED@300). The
syntax-DB global init (C_HL_keywords etc.) eats most of the 200-step budget;
shrinking to numrows=1 does NOT free enough (still survives @200) and makes more
mutants equivalent — not worth it. Same depth-200 cap pattern as siblings
[[cbmc-editorreadkey]], [[cbmc-editorinsertchar]], [[cbmc-editorinsertnewline]].

Scorer: `/app/score_emc.py` (patches each mutant line onto kilo.c, runs the
canonical pipeline, no callee replacement needed). Keep it (don't delete).

1	---
2	name: cbmc-depth-vacuous-and-recursive-callees
3	description: "Two CBMC gotchas in the kilo repo — --depth silently makes postconditions vacuous, and recursive callees crash the contract inliner (fix with a no-op stub body)"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 73f630cf-61d6-46df-869f-3d6224ca55ca
8	---
9	
10	While verifying `editorUpdateRow` in /app/kilo/kilo.c, two non-obvious CBMC issues bit me:
11	
12	**1. `--depth N` makes postconditions vacuously pass.** With `cbmc --depth 400`, every
13	`__CPROVER_ensures` reported SUCCESS even when deliberately set to `row->rsize == 999`.
14	The depth bound truncates the symbolic path *before* reaching the postcondition check, so it
15	is never evaluated → false "VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL" and a near-zero mutation kill score
16	(only mutants failing an *early* bounds check were killed). Fix: drop `--depth` entirely when
17	loops are already bounded by `--unwind` + `--unwinding-assertions`. Always sanity-check that a
18	contract is live by temporarily falsifying an ensures and confirming `postcondition.* : FAILURE`.
19	
20	**2. Recursive callees crash the contract inliner.** `editorUpdateSyntax` self-calls on the
21	`E.syntax!=NULL` path; enforcing a *caller's* contract while inlining it gives
22	"Recursive call ... during inlining / Numeric exception : 0". `goto-instrument
23	--remove-function-body F` does NOT fix it cleanly: a call to the now-bodyless F raises
24	`<caller>.no-body.F: FAILURE`. Working fix: remove kilo's body, then link a separate no-op
25	stub providing F's body so the call returns normally:
26	`goto-cc -o stub.goto stub_F.c` then `goto-cc -o x.goto x.goto stub.goto --function <caller>`.
27	The stub uses an incomplete `typedef struct erow erow;` and never derefs the pointer. This is
28	sound here only because the callee runs *after* all mutated logic. See
29	[[cbmc-isfresh-mutation-kills]].
30	
31	**3. Killing spurious-`exit()` mutants with an exit stub.** Mutants that corrupt the
32	`allocsize > UINT32_MAX` overflow guard into firing (`<`, `<=`, `!=`) call `exit(1)`. CBMC
33	models `exit` as no-return (`assume(false)`), which *prunes* that path, so the postcondition
34	is never checked → the mutant SURVIVES. Under `editorUpdateRow`'s precondition (`row->size==1`)
35	allocsize is a tiny constant, so the guard is provably false and `exit` is unreachable. Linking
36	a stub that models `exit` as `__CPROVER_assert(0); __CPROVER_assume(0);` turns "reaching exit"
37	into a failure: original still verifies (exit unreachable), and the 3 guard-firing mutants are
38	now KILLED. Stub file `stub_exit.c` (defines `void exit(int)`), linked like the editorUpdateSyntax
39	stub. This raised editorUpdateRow's kill score 16/26 → 19/26.
40	
41	**4. The canonical avocado harness (tools/run_cbmc.py) CANNOT score this function.** It runs
42	`--partial-loops --unwind 5 ... --depth 200` with `--replace-call-with-contract editorUpdateSyntax`
43	(NOT the local script's `--unwind 10`, no-depth, remove-body stub, explicit `--*-check` flags).
44	At `--depth 200` everything is vacuous (issue #1) → 0/26. At full depth, `--partial-loops` lets
45	loops fall through over-iterated, so even the ORIGINAL code fails (render[idx] OOB, assigns,
46	postcondition). So depth 200 is what makes canonical proofs "succeed" by truncation. Net: this
47	spec can only be meaningfully scored by the repo's LOCAL scripts, not the canonical harness.
48	The local scripts' `--depth`-free, no-`--partial-loops` run is the real kill-score measure.
49	
50	Result: editorUpdateRow verifies, kill score 19/26 (was 16/26); the 7 survivors are all equivalent
51	mutants (`j != size` ≡ `j < size`; `nonprint` is dead/always 0) or the `allocsize > UINT32_MAX`
52	overflow guard (needs a ~4GB row to exercise — infeasible in BMC).
53	Helper scripts: verify_editorUpdateRow.sh, run_mutants_editorUpdateRow.sh, stub_editorUpdateSyntax.c,
54	stub_exit.c; plus verify_canonical_editorUpdateRow.sh + run_mutants_canonical_editorUpdateRow.sh
55	(demonstrate the canonical-harness 0/26).
56

---
name: cbmc-depth-vacuous-and-recursive-callees
description: "Two CBMC gotchas in the kilo repo — --depth silently makes postconditions vacuous, and recursive callees crash the contract inliner (fix with a no-op stub body)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 73f630cf-61d6-46df-869f-3d6224ca55ca
---

While verifying `editorUpdateRow` in /app/kilo/kilo.c, two non-obvious CBMC issues bit me:

**1. `--depth N` makes postconditions vacuously pass.** With `cbmc --depth 400`, every
`__CPROVER_ensures` reported SUCCESS even when deliberately set to `row->rsize == 999`.
The depth bound truncates the symbolic path *before* reaching the postcondition check, so it
is never evaluated → false "VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL" and a near-zero mutation kill score
(only mutants failing an *early* bounds check were killed). Fix: drop `--depth` entirely when
loops are already bounded by `--unwind` + `--unwinding-assertions`. Always sanity-check that a
contract is live by temporarily falsifying an ensures and confirming `postcondition.* : FAILURE`.

**2. Recursive callees crash the contract inliner.** `editorUpdateSyntax` self-calls on the
`E.syntax!=NULL` path; enforcing a *caller's* contract while inlining it gives
"Recursive call ... during inlining / Numeric exception : 0". `goto-instrument
--remove-function-body F` does NOT fix it cleanly: a call to the now-bodyless F raises
`<caller>.no-body.F: FAILURE`. Working fix: remove kilo's body, then link a separate no-op
stub providing F's body so the call returns normally:
`goto-cc -o stub.goto stub_F.c` then `goto-cc -o x.goto x.goto stub.goto --function <caller>`.
The stub uses an incomplete `typedef struct erow erow;` and never derefs the pointer. This is
sound here only because the callee runs *after* all mutated logic. See
[[cbmc-isfresh-mutation-kills]].

**3. Killing spurious-`exit()` mutants with an exit stub.** Mutants that corrupt the
`allocsize > UINT32_MAX` overflow guard into firing (`<`, `<=`, `!=`) call `exit(1)`. CBMC
models `exit` as no-return (`assume(false)`), which *prunes* that path, so the postcondition
is never checked → the mutant SURVIVES. Under `editorUpdateRow`'s precondition (`row->size==1`)
allocsize is a tiny constant, so the guard is provably false and `exit` is unreachable. Linking
a stub that models `exit` as `__CPROVER_assert(0); __CPROVER_assume(0);` turns "reaching exit"
into a failure: original still verifies (exit unreachable), and the 3 guard-firing mutants are
now KILLED. Stub file `stub_exit.c` (defines `void exit(int)`), linked like the editorUpdateSyntax
stub. This raised editorUpdateRow's kill score 16/26 → 19/26.

**4. The canonical avocado harness (tools/run_cbmc.py) CANNOT score this function.** It runs
`--partial-loops --unwind 5 ... --depth 200` with `--replace-call-with-contract editorUpdateSyntax`
(NOT the local script's `--unwind 10`, no-depth, remove-body stub, explicit `--*-check` flags).
At `--depth 200` everything is vacuous (issue #1) → 0/26. At full depth, `--partial-loops` lets
loops fall through over-iterated, so even the ORIGINAL code fails (render[idx] OOB, assigns,
postcondition). So depth 200 is what makes canonical proofs "succeed" by truncation. Net: this
spec can only be meaningfully scored by the repo's LOCAL scripts, not the canonical harness.
The local scripts' `--depth`-free, no-`--partial-loops` run is the real kill-score measure.

Result: editorUpdateRow verifies, kill score 19/26 (was 16/26); the 7 survivors are all equivalent
mutants (`j != size` ≡ `j < size`; `nonprint` is dead/always 0) or the `allocsize > UINT32_MAX`
overflow guard (needs a ~4GB row to exercise — infeasible in BMC).
Helper scripts: verify_editorUpdateRow.sh, run_mutants_editorUpdateRow.sh, stub_editorUpdateSyntax.c,
stub_exit.c; plus verify_canonical_editorUpdateRow.sh + run_mutants_canonical_editorUpdateRow.sh
(demonstrate the canonical-harness 0/26).

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Mutation-scoring harness for editorMoveCursor.
3	
4	Patches each mutant (by line number) onto the current kilo.c (which already
5	contains the editorMoveCursor contract), runs the canonical CBMC pipeline, and
6	classifies the mutant as KILLED (verification fails) or SURVIVED (succeeds).
7	editorMoveCursor has no callees, so no contract replacement is needed.
8	"""
9	import os, re, subprocess, sys, tempfile, shutil, concurrent.futures
10	
11	KILO = "/app/kilo/kilo.c"
12	MUT = "/tmp/emc_mutants.txt"
13	F = "editorMoveCursor"
14	DEPTH = os.environ.get("DEPTH", "200")
15	
16	def parse_mutants(path):
17	    text = open(path).read()
18	    blocks = text.split("--- original")
19	    muts = []
20	    for b in blocks[1:]:
21	        m = re.search(r'@@ -(\d+)', b)
22	        if not m:
23	            continue
24	        lineno = int(m.group(1))
25	        plus = None
26	        for ln in b.splitlines():
27	            if ln.startswith('+++'):
28	                continue
29	            if ln.startswith('+'):
30	                plus = ln[1:]
31	                break
32	        muts.append((lineno, plus, b.strip()))
33	    return muts
34	
35	def build_source(lineno, mutline):
36	    lines = open(KILO).read().split('\n')
37	    if lineno is not None:
38	        lines[lineno-1] = mutline
39	    return '\n'.join(lines)
40	
41	def run_cbmc(src, workdir):
42	    f = os.path.join(workdir, "m.c")
43	    open(f, 'w').write(src)
44	    g = os.path.join(workdir, "m.goto")
45	    c = os.path.join(workdir, "chk.goto")
46	    def sh(cmd):
47	        return subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=workdir, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120)
48	    r = sh(["goto-cc", "-o", g, f, "--function", F])
49	    if r.returncode != 0:
50	        return ("GOTOCC_ERR", r.stderr[-500:])
51	    sh(["goto-instrument", "--partial-loops", "--unwind", "5", g, g])
52	    sh(["goto-instrument", "--enforce-contract", F, g, c])
53	    try:
54	        r = subprocess.run(["cbmc", c, "--function", F, "--depth", DEPTH],
55	                           cwd=workdir, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=300)
56	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
57	        return ("TIMEOUT", "")
58	    out = r.stdout
59	    if "VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL" in out:
60	        return ("SUCCESS", "")
61	    if "VERIFICATION FAILED" in out:
62	        fails = [l for l in out.splitlines() if ": FAILURE" in l]
63	        return ("FAILED", fails[:3])
64	    return ("OTHER", out[-500:])
65	
66	def classify(args):
67	    lineno, mutline, diff = args
68	    d = tempfile.mkdtemp()
69	    try:
70	        src = build_source(lineno, mutline)
71	        verdict, info = run_cbmc(src, d)
72	    finally:
73	        shutil.rmtree(d, ignore_errors=True)
74	    return (lineno, mutline, verdict, info)
75	
76	def main():
77	    muts = parse_mutants(MUT)
78	    d = tempfile.mkdtemp()
79	    base_v, base_i = run_cbmc(build_source(None, None), d)
80	    shutil.rmtree(d, ignore_errors=True)
81	    print(f"BASELINE (original): {base_v} {base_i if base_v!='SUCCESS' else ''}")
82	    if base_v != "SUCCESS":
83	        print("Baseline does not verify; aborting scoring.")
84	        return
85	    tasks = [(ln, ml, df) for (ln, ml, df) in muts]
86	    killed = survived = other = 0
87	    survivors = []
88	    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=8) as ex:
89	        for (lineno, mutline, verdict, info) in ex.map(classify, tasks):
90	            if verdict == "FAILED":
91	                killed += 1
92	                tag = "KILL"
93	            elif verdict == "SUCCESS":
94	                survived += 1
95	                survivors.append((lineno, mutline.strip()))
96	                tag = "SURV"
97	            else:
98	                other += 1
99	                survivors.append((lineno, mutline.strip() + f"  [{verdict}]"))
100	                tag = verdict
101	            print(f"  [{tag}] L{lineno}: {mutline.strip()[:80]}")
102	    print(f"\nKILLED={killed} SURVIVED={survived} OTHER={other} TOTAL={len(muts)}")
103	    print("Survivors:")
104	    for ln, ml in sorted(survivors):
105	        print(f"  L{ln}: {ml[:90]}")
106	
107	if __name__ == "__main__":

the score 34→23. The giant nested-ternary ensures evaluation consumes the shared

so fewer mutants are observed. Cheaper, well-chosen ensures beat exhaustive ones.

[[cbmc-editorreadkey]], [[cbmc-editorinsertchar]], [[cbmc-editorinsertnewline]].

content.text:
Done. Summary:

**editorMoveCursor already verified** (canonical `--partial-loops --unwind 5 --depth 200`, baseline SUCCESS), so I worked on strengthening the spec.

**Result: kill score improved 34/61 → 37/61** with a single change — reordering the postconditions so the exact-`rowoff` ensures comes **before** the exact-`filerow` ensures.

Why it works: at `--depth 200` the global syntax-DB init consumes most of the step budget, so whichever heavy ensures is evaluated *last* gets truncated on the longest body paths (ARROW_RIGHT/DOWN) before its assertion is reached, and loses its unique kills. The exact-`rowoff` ensures is the killer for the 4 `screenrows±1` mutants (L2002×2, L2018×2); moving it out of last place lets all 4 die at depth 200 (previously only killable at depth ≥400). The trade is that `filerow`, now last, loses exactly 1 kill (L1979 `filerow < 0`) — net **+3**.

Things I tried that backfired (documented in memory so I don't repeat them):
- Removing the load-bearing `coloff+cx <= rowlen` ensures → 50 mutants go OTHER.
- Adding a dedicated cheap ARROW_LEFT filerow ensures to recover the lost kill → pushes CBMC over the per-mutant timeout → 50 OTHER.
